content.assessment: Poland's 2025 draft legislation is directly cited as a mechanism precedent, proposing repurposing rather than binary closure and framing rural schools as social/cultural centres — this is weak-strength evidence (draft, not yet implemented or evaluated) but directly relevant to the hub-conversion component of the proposal. The contested cross-national evidence on closure-and-population-decline (Polish village studies finding smaller social-capital decline where schools were saved, versus mixed Finnish/Swedish findings) is directly relevant to the hub/cluster retention rationale, but the contested nature must be preserved: this is not settled evidence that networked retention reliably preserves social capital or population stability, only a plausible mechanism supported unevenly across contexts.
opportunities: If implemented and evaluated, Poland's 2025 repurposing model could offer a comparator case for assessing whether hub conversion outperforms simple closure in preserving community function
strengths: Poland's 2025 draft legislation provides an emerging precedent for infrastructure repurposing as an alternative to binary closure, aligning with the hub-conversion component of the proposal (F-live-polish_reform-06)

Polish village-level studies found smaller structural social-capital decline where schools were saved, offering some support for the retention rationale underlying networked clustering (F-live-polish_reform-07)
threats: Reliance on a mechanism with contested cross-national evidence risks overstating confidence in networked retention's ability to prevent the social-capital and population-decline harms the proposal aims to avoid (F-live-polish_reform-07)
weaknesses: The Polish 2025 evidence is weak-strength and drawn from draft legislation not yet implemented or evaluated, limiting confidence in its predictive value

Cross-national evidence on the closure-population decline link is explicitly contested (Finnish and Swedish findings diverge from Polish findings), so the mechanism cannot be assumed to transfer reliably (F-live-polish_reform-07)
